markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define checks that catch direct and transitive SQLite usage in code, tests, fixtures, caches, examples, and temp adapters touched by WP-009. This MT belongs to NativeDependencyAndPackaging. Make WP-009 native-by-default and fully packaged inside Handshake.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
No proof may require manually launched external services or tools except explicitly allowed model/GGUF/tensor/CUI-portable surfaces.
Bundled frontend/editor libraries must be lockfile-governed, license-reviewed, and callable through Handshake UI/runtime surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: NoSQLiteTransitiveRegression leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: NoSQLiteTransitiveRegression is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: Implemented in wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commit 304dfdee; proof commands green per delegate report (vitest/cargo/playwright; MT-032 aggregate check:dependency-policy 8/8 pass, app suite 313/313). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: Strong multi-layer design: npm manifest scan + pnpm-lock hard scan (any name containing 'sqlite') + cargo direct-declaration scan (incl alias=package rename) + cargo-tree --all-features -i inertness proof + Rust feature-aware activation closure over cargo metadata. The dormant sqlx-sqlite union is correctly proven INERT (4 advisories, all inert) rather than false-positived.

node:sqlite source scan inherits the blanket dependency_policy path exemption (harness exempt) and the SOURCE_EXTENSIONS limitation (a .wasm sqlite or a non-listed-extension file would not be content-scanned) - but the manifest/lock/activation layers cover the realistic package vectors, so risk is low.

cargoTreeProvesAbsent treats 'did not match any packages' as proof of absence - correct, but if cargo itself errored for an unrelated reason the broad catch could mask it; it does re-throw other errors, so acceptable.
evasion_probes: Feature-smuggling (sqlx features=['sqlite']) IS flagged by forbidden_activated_packages (test feature_smuggled_sqlite_is_flagged); renamed-feature dep:rusqlite IS flagged; alias=package rename IS caught by the direct scan.
Dormant union (sqlx postgres-only) correctly NOT flagged (dormant_optional_sqlite_union_is_not_flagged + real-graph inertness pass).
evidence_checked: pnpm run check:dependency-policy ran end-to-end with REAL builds (tsc+vite app/dist + build:harness app/dist-harness); exit 0; 8/8 checks pass. forbidden-manifest-scan pass + sqlite-cargo-inertness pass (libsqlite3-sys & sqlx-sqlite inert in BOTH lockfiles).

vitest 56/56 green across app/src/lib/dependency_policy/*, DependencyFailureBanner.test.tsx, worker_map.test.ts, extension_set.test.ts. no_sqlite_regression.test.ts (runs real cargo tree, checked>=2).

evidence: Implementation commit 304dfdee on feat/WP-KERNEL-009; adversarial code review verdict HARDEN_RECOMMENDED -> remediated in commit 0bfcceb2, exploits independently re-run by reviewer and confirmed CLOSED; runtime evidence: check:dependency-policy 8/8 exit 0 (real builds), cargo --lib dependency_policy:: 31/31, vitest 67/67 post-hardening.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
